notes: Midnight-2am: minimal orders; only automated/scheduled shipments
2-4am: lowest demand window; night shift warehouse processing
4-6am: early morning processing begins; overnight orders dispatched
6-8am: workers arrive; morning order wave begins (India wakes up early)
8-10am: strong morning peak; office commuters placing orders
10am-12pm: primary peak window; Flipkart/Amazon highest order volume
12-2pm: lunch break ordering spike; food + quick commerce surge
2-4pm: post-lunch dip; steady background demand
4-6pm: pre-evening ramp; office workers browsing
6-8pm: PRIMARY PEAK — highest volume of day; post-work shopping surge
8-10pm: HIGHEST PEAK — India's prime time shopping window; Meesho/Flipkart flash sales
10pm-midnight: declining but still significant; last-minute orders
Weekend midnight: slightly higher than weekday (late night shoppers)
Weekend 2-4am: lowest of weekend cycle
Weekend early morning: higher than weekday (no early commute)
Weekend morning: slower ramp (people sleep in)
Weekend 8-10am: family shopping begins; grocery + lifestyle orders
Weekend 10am-12pm: peak weekend browsing window
Weekend lunch: highest midday demand (families at home)
Weekend afternoon: sustained high demand; leisure shopping
Weekend 4-6pm: pre-evening high; festival/gifting orders peak
Weekend evening: social commerce; group orders; gifting
Weekend prime time: ABSOLUTE HIGHEST; Big Billion Day type demand
Weekend late evening: sustained vs weekday equivalent
Diwali/Big Billion Day/Republic Day Sale: midnight launch surge
Festival morning: queued orders from overnight; warehouse crunch
Festival mid-morning: MAXIMUM DEMAND — flash sale peak hours
Festival afternoon: sustained surge; cart checkouts
Festival evening: second wave; gifting + personal orders
Festival prime time: absolute maximum; system stress test
Sunday night: weekly inventory replenishment orders flood in
Monday morning: weekend order processing + new week demand
Month-end (25th-31st): salary credited; aspirational purchase surge
Month-end mid-morning: highest salary-effect demand window
Amazon/Flipkart sale: midnight deal unlock surge
Sale event peak hours: deal hunters + impulse buyers combined
